issue_title: Structured output parser — extract files from phase output
issue_description: Parse === FILE: path === ... === END FILE === blocks from LLM output into (path, content) pairs. New module output_parser.py. Path traversal protection. Backward compatible — no markers means raw text returned as-is.
acceptance_criteria: Function parse_output(text: str) -> ParsedOutput that extracts FILE blocks
ParsedOutput has: raw_text (str), files (list of FileBlock with path and content), has_files (bool)
FileBlock dataclass with path: str, content: str
Path traversal protection: reject paths with .., absolute paths, or paths outside allowed directory
Backward compatible: text without FILE markers returns ParsedOutput with raw_text=input, files=[], has_files=False
Handles edge cases: empty file blocks, multiple files, nested code fences inside file blocks
Regex-based parsing with === FILE: <path> === and === END FILE === delimiters
Optional: function extract_and_write(text: str, output_dir: Path) -> writes extracted files to disk
codebase_context: The orchestration engine currently treats all phase output as raw text. The sequencer stores phase output in phase_outputs dict as strings. The OpenClaw executor extracts output from sub-agent sessions as text. This module sits between the executor output and the sequencer storage — it parses structured file blocks from LLM output so that coding pipelines can extract actual code files from phase results. The module should be standalone with no dependencies on other engine modules.
constraints: Pure Python stdlib only — no new dependencies
Dataclass-based API (no Pydantic for this module)
Path traversal protection is MANDATORY — this will write files to disk
Must handle Windows-style paths (backslash) in the traversal check
File content should preserve exact whitespace/newlines (no stripping)
The FILE delimiter format is: === FILE: relative/path.py ===
The END delimiter format is: === END FILE ===
